title: Reduce beads instruction redundancy in CLAUDE.md files
description: Beads workflow instructions appear in 3 places (~105 lines total): bd prime --stealth hook output (~80 lines), global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md Task Management section (~15 lines), and streams/CLAUDE.md Task Tracking section (~10 lines). Since bd prime is auto-injected by hooks, slim down both CLAUDE.md entries to just reference the bd prime output. Replace with: 'Use beads (bd) for all task tracking. Do not use TaskCreate, TaskList, TaskUpdate, or MEMORY.md. See bd prime output for full command reference.' Saves ~20 lines of context per conversation.
close_reason: Slimmed both CLAUDE.md files. Global removed 8 lines of redundant bd commands; streams CLAUDE.md removed 9 lines. Kept unique content (wrapping up session protocol, stealth mode note).

title: Add delete stream feature to TUI
description: Completed streams remain in the dashboard with no way to remove them. Add a 'd' keybinding with confirmation overlay that removes stream data from disk/memory but leaves git branch and beads issue intact. See docs/plans/cuddly-rolling-gray.md for full plan.
close_reason: Implemented Store.Delete, Orchestrator.Delete (with worktree cleanup), and TUI delete confirmation overlay with d keybinding. Verified via tmux: confirmation shows, n/esc cancels, y deletes and removes stream from list.

title: Dashboard cursor/sort mismatch causes wrong stream selection
description: selectedStream() in app.go uses unsorted orch.List() (map iteration order) while renderDashboard() sorts by CreatedAt. The cursor index maps to different streams in each context, so Enter, Start, Stop, and Guidance all operate on the wrong stream.
close_reason: Sorted List() results by CreatedAt in orchestrator; removed redundant sort from renderDashboard

title: Decide minimum viable scope for v1 single stream
description: To get a single stream running end-to-end, we can likely defer several features. Proposed v1 scope: (1) INCLUDE: Runtime interface, Claude CLI runtime, Loop engine, Coding phase only. (2) DEFER: Plan and Decompose phases (manually create step beads), TUI (run headless with stdout logging), persistence (in-memory only), quality gates (just convergence detection), guidance injection. The critical path is: Runtime -> Loop engine -> Coding phase -> single stream run. Need to confirm this scope and adjust the implementation task dependency chain accordingly.
close_reason: V1 scope confirmed: Runtime + Loop + Coding phase only, headless (no TUI), in-memory (no persistence), no guidance injection. Deferred: Plan/Decompose phases, TUI, persistence, quality gates, guidance. Dependency chain set up: scaffolding → runtime types + JSON docs → CLI runtime + loop engine → main entry point → smoke test. Issue descriptions updated to reflect v1 scope.

title: Remove redundant bd close permission from settings.local.json
description: Bash(bd close:*) in streams/.claude/settings.local.json is unnecessary because Bash(bd *) in the global ~/.claude/settings.json already covers all bd commands. Remove the redundant entry from settings.local.json.
close_reason: Removed Bash(bd close:*) from streams/.claude/settings.local.json — redundant since global settings.json has Bash(bd *).

title: Implement default quality gates and wire into loop
description: gates.go has only the Gate interface — no concrete implementations. Need to: (1) implement default gates (pattern conformance, simplicity, readability, hindsight check) that parse review text for structured signals, (2) wire gate evaluation into the loop at StepCheckpoint so GateResults are populated in each snapshot. The TUI detail view already renders GateResults — this just needs data.
close_reason: Implemented 4 default gates (pattern-conformance, simplicity, readability, hindsight) using a shared keywordGate type, wired evaluation into loop at StepCheckpoint to populate snap.GateResults, added comprehensive tests in gates_test.go, and fixed loop_test.go to match updated Run signature with onCheckpoint parameter. All tests pass.

title: Debug app startup capability
description:
The app requires GOTOOLCHAIN=auto due to Bubble Tea v2 needing Go 1.24.2+ while the system has Go 1.23.1.

To start the app:

```
GOTOOLCHAIN=auto go run ./cmd/streams/
```

Key bindings once running:
- n — create a new stream (type a task, press enter)
- j/k — navigate the stream list
- s — start the selected stream (requires claude CLI in PATH)
- enter — inspect a stream's snapshots
- esc — back to dashboard
- q — quit

Need to investigate and fix startup issues so the app runs reliably without workarounds.
close_reason: Closed

title: Smoke test the loop against a real repo
description: Manual validation of v1 headless mode. Run streams against a small Go project with a well-defined task (e.g. add HTTP handler). Pre-create step beads manually to simulate decompose output. Verify: (1) CLI invoked with correct --allowedTools for coding phase, (2) JSON output parsed correctly, (3) Review agent files child beads issues, (4) Implement agent reads children and closes them, (5) Convergence detected when review files zero new issues, (6) Worktree created and commits land on stream branch, (7) Autosquash works after review feedback, (8) Ctrl+C clean exit, (9) Cost logged per iteration to stderr.
close_reason: Smoke test passed. Fixed two bugs in beads.go: (1) JSON parsing expected flat array but bd returns map keyed by parent ID, (2) sequence stored in notes field not metadata. Validated: worktree creation, implement step closing beads, review step filing new issues, autosquash collapsing fixups, max-iterations exit, cost logging.

title: Establish high-level project requirements
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement TUI package (Bubble Tea)
description: Build the Bubble Tea TUI per ARCHITECTURE.md. Three views: dashboard (stream list with cursor), detail (snapshot list + selected snapshot details), guidance overlay (textarea). Key bindings: j/k navigation, enter to inspect, n new stream, s start/resume, x stop, g guidance, esc/q back/quit. Receives orchestrator Events via EventSink interface to update in real time. All code in internal/ui/ (app.go, dashboard.go, detail.go, styles.go).
close_reason: Implemented all four TUI files: app.go (root model + EventSink), dashboard.go, detail.go, styles.go. Bubble Tea v1.2.4 + Lip Gloss v1.0.0 + Bubbles v0.20.0.

title: Error block renders twice in detail view
description: When a stream has an error, the detail view shows the error block both in the header area (from st.LastError) and in the snapshot detail panel (from snap.Error). This is redundant. The snapshot error display should be sufficient, or the header error should only show when viewing a snapshot that doesn't have the error.
close_reason: Removed the top-level st.LastError error block from renderDetail. The error is still shown in the snapshot detail panel via snap.Error, eliminating the duplicate display.

title: Autosquash fails when worktree has unstaged changes from implement step
description: The coding phase's BeforeReview (autosquash) runs git rebase --autosquash which fails with 'cannot rebase: You have unstaged changes'. This happens because the implement step agent may modify files without committing them (especially on first iteration with no child steps from decompose). Either skip autosquash when there are no fixup commits, or stash before rebase.
close_reason: Fixed BeforeReview to handle dirty worktrees: (1) skip rebase entirely when HEAD==BaseSHA (no commits to squash), (2) stash/pop uncommitted changes around the rebase when HEAD\!=BaseSHA but worktree is dirty.

title: Define Runtime interface and types
description: Create internal/runtime/runtime.go with Request (Prompt + Options map), Response (Text + CostUSD), and Runtime interface (Run method only). No SessionID — fresh session per invocation. Stream()/Event deferred to pairing mode.
close_reason: Created internal/runtime/runtime.go with Request, Response, and Runtime interface

title: Explore live Claude TUI attach for tail view
description:
The current tail view (t key in detail view) shows read-only output lines from stream-json parsing. User wants to explore putting the user directly into the live Claude Code TUI so they can observe and interact with the underlying claude instance.

Two options to explore:

**Option A: --resume for attach/detach**
- Keep -p mode for the orchestrator (structured I/O for parsing)
- When user presses t, suspend bubbletea and exec 'claude --resume <session-id>' interactively
- User gets the full Claude Code TUI for that session
- When they quit claude, bubbletea resumes
- Tradeoff: resuming starts a new turn — changes happen outside orchestrator awareness
- Session ID is already available from stream-json result events

**Option B: PTY-based live view**
- Run claude interactively (no -p) inside a PTY (e.g. creack/pty)
- Orchestrator feeds prompts via PTY stdin, reads terminal output
- User 'attaches' to see the real TUI (like tmux attach)
- Tradeoff: lose structured JSON parsing — would need to parse terminal escape codes or find another way to track orchestration state

**Key files:**
- internal/runtime/claude/claude.go (runStreaming - currently uses -p --verbose --output-format stream-json)
- internal/runtime/claude/response.go (event types, session_id captured in result event)
- internal/loop/loop.go (orchestrator loop, OnOutput wiring)
- internal/ui/tail.go (current tail view rendering)
- internal/ui/app.go (bubbletea app, handles t key)

**Prior context:**
- streams-u0x (closed) fixed the stream-json parsing (added --verbose, --include-partial-messages, stream_event handler)
- The current tail view works for showing buffered output lines but is not a live-tail and has no user input
- Commit cfd7568 has the runtime parsing fix
close_reason: Exploration complete. Option A (--resume via tea.ExecProcess) selected. Option B (PTY) deferred — too complex, loses structured JSON. Implementation filed as streams-4k6 -> streams-4ly -> streams-lwp. Plan at docs/plans/valiant-gathering-fern.md

title: Sequence metadata vs notes field: reconcile docs and code
description: ARCHITECTURE.md specifies metadata.sequence for step ordering, but the actual implementation in decompose.go and beads.go uses the notes field with 'sequence:N' format. The decompose prompt templates need to match what FetchOrderedSteps actually parses. Either update the code to use metadata (if beads supports it) or update ARCHITECTURE.md to reflect the notes-based approach. Current approach works but the discrepancy could cause confusion.

title: Runtime.Stream() method for real-time pairing interaction
description: ARCHITECTURE.md notes that Stream() for pairing mode will be added later. Currently Runtime only has Run() which blocks for a complete response. Pairing mode needs a streaming/interactive method on the Runtime interface so the user can have a real-time conversation within a stream, not just attach to a finished session.

title: Implement the iteration loop with pluggable macro-phases
description: Create internal/loop/loop.go and internal/loop/phase.go. The loop engine runs implement → autosquash → review → convergence check → checkpoint cycles. It is macro-phase agnostic — each MacroPhase provides prompts, tools, and convergence criteria. Implement the MacroPhase interface and the Coding phase only (loop/coding.go). Plan and Decompose phases are deferred — users manually create step beads. Pipeline is hardcoded to ['coding'] for v1. No guidance injection (deferred). No persistence (in-memory snapshots only). Headless operation — log iteration progress, convergence, and errors to stderr via slog. See ARCHITECTURE.md Loop section for full design.
close_reason: All subtasks complete: stream types, loop interfaces, coding phase, loop engine, tests

title: Clarify step beads vs review beads in the coding phase
description: The decompose phase creates step beads as children of the parent. The coding review also creates children of the parent. When the implement agent runs bd show --children, how does it distinguish unfinished steps from review feedback? Temporal ordering works in the happy path but breaks if the first pass doesn't close all steps. Proposed resolution: the implement prompt just says 'address all open children' regardless of origin — the agent doesn't need to distinguish them. Validate this approach and document the decision.
close_reason: Resolved: unified the two coding implement prompts (first-pass vs refinement) into a single prompt. The agent determines commit strategy from child issue content — step beads get regular commits, review feedback gets fixup commits. No need for the loop to track pass type.

title: Thread session_id through runtime Response
description: Add SessionID string field to runtime.Response (runtime.go). Populate from cliResult.SessionID in both runJSON and runStreaming return values (claude.go). Add SessionID assertions to 3 existing tests (claude_test.go). Plan: docs/plans/valiant-gathering-fern.md Step 1
close_reason: SessionID added to runtime.Response and populated in both runJSON/runStreaming paths. Tests pass.

title: Implement coding macro-phase
description: Create internal/loop/coding.go. CodingPhase struct implementing MacroPhase interface. Name() returns 'coding'. ImplementPrompt/ReviewPrompt use exact templates from ARCHITECTURE.md Prompt Design section — implement prompt includes ordered steps placeholder filled by PhaseContext.OrderedSteps, review prompt references parent beads ID for filing child issues. ImplementTools returns [Bash,Read,Edit,Write,Glob,Grep]. ReviewTools returns [Bash,Read,Glob,Grep]. IsConverged returns result.OpenChildrenAfter <= result.OpenChildrenBefore. BeforeReview runs 'git rebase --autosquash <BaseSHA>' in the worktree, aborts rebase on failure. TransitionMode returns TransitionPause. See ARCHITECTURE.md Coding phase section for full prompt templates.
close_reason: Implemented coding.go with prompts, tools, convergence, autosquash

title: Store session_id on Stream from loop
description: Add SessionID field + GetSessionID/SetSessionID to stream.Stream (stream.go). After each rt.Run() in loop.go, store resp.SessionID on the stream (guard on nil resp). Add TestRunStoresSessionID (loop_test.go). Plan: docs/plans/valiant-gathering-fern.md Step 2
close_reason: SessionID stored on Stream after implement step. Getter/setter pair added. Test passes.

title: Clean stale beads lock files
description: bd doctor reports 2 stale lock files (~86 hours old) from a crashed Dolt process: .beads/dolt-access.lock and .beads/dolt/beads_streams/.dolt/noms/LOCK. Fix by running bd doctor --fix.
close_reason: Removed stale dolt-access.lock via bd doctor --fix --yes. The noms/LOCK file is a live lock recreated by dolt on each database access — not stale, likely a bd doctor false positive.

title: Initialize Go module and project scaffolding
description: Create go.mod, directory structure (cmd/streams/, internal/runtime/, internal/runtime/claude/, internal/loop/), Makefile with build/test/run targets, and a stub main.go.
close_reason: go.mod, cmd/streams/main.go, Makefile, .gitignore, and directory structure created

title: Configurable quality gates
description: R3 in REQUIREMENTS.md says gates are configurable — the user should be able to tune what the agent evaluates. Currently gates are hardcoded in DefaultGates() with fixed keyword lists. Discuss: per-project config file? Per-stream override? Is keyword-based evaluation sufficient or should gates evolve to something richer?

title: Wire main.go to launch TUI with orchestrator
description: Replace the direct loop invocation in cmd/streams/main.go with tea.NewProgram(ui.New(orch)). Create Store and Orchestrator, connect EventSink, call LoadExisting on startup. Preserve current headless mode behind a --headless flag.
close_reason: Rewrote main.go to create Store/Orchestrator, launch TUI by default, preserved headless mode behind --headless flag

title: Add stream types package (stream.go, snapshot.go, errors.go)
description: Create internal/stream/ with three files. IMPORTANT: LoopError and ErrorKind live in stream package (not loop) to avoid circular imports since Stream.LastError is *LoopError. See ARCHITECTURE.md Stream State Model section for exact field definitions. Files: (1) errors.go — ErrorKind enum (ErrRuntime, ErrBudget, ErrAutosquash, ErrNoProgress, ErrInfra) and LoopError struct (Kind, Step, Message, Detail) implementing error interface. (2) snapshot.go — Snapshot struct (Phase, Iteration, Summary, Review, GateResults, CostUSD, DiffStat, CommitSHAs, BeadsClosed, BeadsOpened, GuidanceConsumed, Error, Timestamp), Guidance struct (Text, Timestamp), GateResult struct (Gate, Passed, Detail). (3) stream.go — IterStep enum (StepImplement..StepGuidance), Mode enum (ModeAutonomous, ModePairing), Status enum (StatusCreated..StatusStopped) all with String() methods. Stream struct with sync.RWMutex and all fields from ARCHITECTURE.md. Thread-safe methods: SetStatus, GetStatus, SetIterStep, SetIteration, GetIteration, SetConverged, SetLastError, AppendSnapshot. See plan at ~/.claude/plans/cheeky-spinning-barto.md
close_reason: Implemented errors.go, snapshot.go, stream.go per ARCHITECTURE.md

title: Test stream two
close_reason: Test streams created during TUI dogfooding, not real work

title: Define snapshot content and what gets stored from CLI output
description: The Snapshot struct has Summary and Review string fields. But Claude CLI JSON output for a full session can be enormous (every tool call, file read, etc). Need to decide what goes into Summary. Options: (1) full CLI JSON response — too large, (2) just final text output — loses tool call detail, (3) structured summary extracted from JSON by the Go loop. This affects persistence size, TUI display capabilities, and debugging. Also: should the Snapshot record which macro-phase it belongs to? Currently it only has Iteration but no phase context.
design:
## Snapshot Content Design

### Snapshot struct

```go
type Snapshot struct {
    Phase       string
    Iteration   int
    Summary     string       // CLI result text (final text output from claude -p --output-format json)
    Review      string       // reviewer's text output
    GateResults []GateResult
    CostUSD     float64
    DiffStat    string       // git diff --stat output (coding phase only)
    CommitSHAs  []string     // commits made this iteration (coding phase only)
    BeadsClosed []string     // bead IDs closed by implement step
    BeadsOpened []string     // bead IDs opened by review step
    Timestamp   time.Time
}
```

### Key decisions

- **Summary source**: Use the `result` field from `--output-format json`. Claude naturally summarizes its work at the end of each invocation, so this is compact and informative without storing the full tool-call trace.
- **No full diffs stored**: Snapshots store commit SHAs and diffstat only. Full diffs are rendered live via `git show <sha>` when the user drills down. Keeps snapshots small.
- **No full CLI JSON**: The complete JSON output (every tool call, file read, etc.) is discarded. The `result` text is sufficient for the "what happened" view.
- **Phase field added**: Each snapshot records which macro-phase produced it, enabling the iteration list to show "Plan 1, Plan 2, Decompose 1, Coding 1..." across the full stream history.
- **Beads delta**: Track IDs opened/closed per iteration for quick "work done / work remaining" signal.

### TUI layout

**Dashboard view** — one row per stream, showing: name, current phase, iteration count. Minimal.

**Detail view** — two-pane layout:
- Left: vertical list of all snapshots across all phases (e.g., "Plan 1", "Plan 2", "Decompose 1", "Coding 1"...). v1 highlights the latest; future adds j/k traversal.
- Right: selected snapshot's details (summary, review, diffstat, gates).
- Keybind `d` (future) could run `git show` on commit SHAs for full diff drill-down.

### What's NOT in v1
- Storing full diffs in snapshots
- Storing full CLI JSON trace
- Plan-phase diffs of plan.md (just read the current file)
- Navigating previous snapshots (v1 shows latest only)
close_reason: Design decided: snapshots store CLI result text + commit SHAs + diffstat + beads delta. No full diffs or CLI JSON. TUI uses two-pane layout in detail view.

title: Decompose phase prompt uses --metadata but code parses --notes for sequence
description: The decompose phase implement prompt tells the agent to use 'bd create --metadata {"sequence":N}' but the Go code in loop/beads.go parseSequence() looks for 'sequence:N' in the notes field. This mismatch means step ordering won't work. Either update the prompt to use '--notes sequence:N' or update parseSequence to parse metadata JSON.
close_reason: Fixed decompose prompt to use --notes "sequence:N" instead of --metadata '{"sequence":N}', aligning with parseSequence() in beads.go which parses notes. Also updated review prompt references from metadata.sequence to notes sequence, and updated decompose_test.go assertions to match. Build and tests pass.

title: Implement git worktree functionality in the current repo. so that when a new stream is opened, it is opened in a new worktree
close_reason: Worktree creation is already implemented in orchestrator.go createWorktree(). Verified working during integration testing.

title: Fix parseBeadsID picking up non-ID fields from bd create output
description: parseBeadsID returns the first field containing a hyphen, but bd create output includes warnings and recommendations with hyphens, causing stream IDs like --description=Why. Should match the beads prefix pattern instead.
close_reason: Fixed parseBeadsID to use --json flag and parse structured output instead of fragile text matching. Cleaned up broken worktree/branch/stream data.

title: Design architecture
description: Design the architecture for Streams. Three areas to cover: (1) Agent runtime interface — pluggable abstraction, Claude Code CLI as first impl. (2) Stream state model — stream lifecycle, loop phases, checkpoints, persistence format. (3) TUI layout — dashboard view, stream detail view, interaction flows (observe, guide, pair, stop/start). Reference REQUIREMENTS.md for full context.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add loop tests
description: Create internal/loop/loop_test.go. Mock types: mockRuntime (configurable per-call responses and errors via a results slice, defaults to successful response when exhausted), mockBeads (configurable per-call open counts via a slice, returns configured steps). Test cases: (1) TestRunConvergesOnFirstIteration — open counts [2,0,0], verify StatusPaused, Converged=true, 1 snapshot, no error. (2) TestRunMultipleIterations — open counts [3,1,3,3,0,0] (iter0 not converged since 3>1, iter1 converged since 0<=0), verify 2 snapshots, iteration=1. (3) TestRunContextCancellation — pre-cancelled context, verify StatusStopped. (4) TestRunRuntimeError — mock returns error on first call, verify StatusPaused, LastError.Kind=ErrRuntime. Run 'go test ./internal/loop/' and 'go vet ./...' to verify.
close_reason: All 4 tests pass: converge, multi-iteration, cancellation, runtime error

title: Fix SendGuidance thread safety
description: orchestrator.SendGuidance (orchestrator.go:239) does direct field append without acquiring the stream mutex: st.Guidance = append(st.Guidance, g). The loop goroutine reads this field concurrently via DrainGuidance. Need to add an AddGuidance method to Stream that locks the mutex, and call it from SendGuidance.
close_reason: Added AddGuidance method to Stream with mutex protection, updated SendGuidance in orchestrator to use it instead of direct field append. All tests pass.

title: Add loop types, interfaces, and beads querier
description: Create internal/loop/ with three files. (1) phase.go — Transition enum (TransitionPause, TransitionAutoAdvance), PhaseContext struct (Stream *stream.Stream, Runtime runtime.Runtime, WorkDir string, Iteration int, OrderedSteps string), IterationResult struct (ReviewText, OpenChildrenBefore, OpenChildrenAfter, BeadsClosed, BeadsOpened), MacroPhase interface (Name, ImplementPrompt, ReviewPrompt, ImplementTools, ReviewTools, IsConverged, BeforeReview, TransitionMode). (2) gates.go — Gate interface (Name() string, Evaluate(reviewText string) stream.GateResult). (3) beads.go — Step struct (ID, Title, Sequence), BeadsQuerier interface (CountOpenChildren(parentID) (int, error), FetchOrderedSteps(parentID) ([]Step, error)), CLIBeadsQuerier struct with WorkDir that shells out to bd CLI. CountOpenChildren runs 'bd show <parent> --children' and counts lines starting with open markers. FetchOrderedSteps runs 'bd show <parent> --children --json', parses JSON, filters open, sorts by metadata.sequence. FormatSteps renders numbered list like '1. streams-abc — Title'. See plan at ~/.claude/plans/cheeky-spinning-barto.md
close_reason: Implemented phase.go, gates.go, beads.go per plan

title: Define convergence detection mechanics and IterationResult type
description: The architecture describes two convergence mechanisms in different places: (1) parsing review output for 'No further improvements needed' and (2) counting open children before/after review via bd show. These should be unified. Proposed: Go loop counts open children before and after the review step — if count didn't increase, phase converged. The prompt string is guidance to the agent, not parsed by Go. Also need to define the IterationResult type referenced in MacroPhase.IsConverged() — should contain at minimum: review agent response text, open-children-before count, open-children-after count.
close_reason: Defined IterationResult type with ReviewText, OpenChildrenBefore/After, BeadsClosed, BeadsOpened. Unified convergence: Go loop counts open children before/after review step, passes delta to IsConverged(). Clarified 'No further improvements needed' is agent guidance, not parsed by Go.

title: Queued guidance lost on TUI restart
description: Guidance queued via the TUI (AddGuidance) is not persisted to disk. If the TUI restarts before the loop drains the guidance, it is lost. The store's streamData struct doesn't include Guidance. Should persist guidance to stream.json and restore it on load.
close_reason: Added Guidance field to streamData, guidanceData struct, and GetGuidance getter; guidance now round-trips through save/load

title: Start error silently dropped in dashboard handler
description: In updateDashboard (app.go:168), m.orch.Start(st.ID) return value is ignored. If Start fails (e.g., phase creation error, stream not found), the user gets no feedback. Should show a status message with the error.
close_reason: Captured Start() error return in both updateDashboard and updateDetail, surfacing via setStatus

title: Implement Decompose macro-phase
description: Add DecomposePhase implementing the MacroPhase interface. Full prompt specs are in ARCHITECTURE.md under 'Decompose phase'. Implement reads plan.md and creates sequenced child beads with metadata. Review checks scoping/ordering/gaps. Tools: both get Bash,Read,Glob,Grep (no code writing). TransitionMode returns auto-advance.
close_reason: DecomposePhase implemented with implement/review prompts, read-only tools (Bash,Read,Glob,Grep), auto-advance transition, and convergence check. Registered in NewPhase factory. All 26 loop tests pass.

title: Test stream one
close_reason: Test streams created during TUI dogfooding, not real work

title: Handle untracked streams directory
description: git status shows ?? streams - an untracked directory at the repo root that creates noise in git status. Either add it to .gitignore or commit it depending on its purpose. User needs to decide what this directory is for.
close_reason: Created .gitignore with 'streams' entry. The streams file is a compiled binary (~5.9MB) that should not be tracked.

title: Detail view 'q' key behavior undocumented in help bar
description: In the detail view, pressing 'q' goes back to dashboard (same as esc), but the help bar only says 'esc: back'. Either document 'q' in the help text or differentiate behavior (q could quit the app entirely, matching dashboard convention).
close_reason: Updated detail view help bar from 'esc: back' to 'q/esc: back'

title: Dashboard help text disappears when streams exist
description: The help text bar (j/k: navigate, enter: inspect, etc.) renders correctly on the empty dashboard but disappears once a stream is created. The renderDashboard function includes it unconditionally after the stream list, but it doesn't appear in the rendered output. Only the title and stream rows render.
close_reason: Fixed by separating help text from dashboard body and using layoutWithFooter to pin it to the bottom of the terminal. The root cause was that the dashboard View output was compact (only as tall as the content), while overlays filled the full terminal height via lipgloss.Place. Transitioning between these different-height outputs caused the help text to disappear. The fix ensures the dashboard always fills the full terminal height with the help bar pinned at the bottom.

title: Implement Claude CLI runtime
description: Create internal/runtime/claude/claude.go. Build CLI args from Request (--allowedTools, --append-system-prompt, --permission-mode acceptEdits, --max-budget-usd, --output-format json). Run via exec.CommandContext. Parse stdout JSON array, find type=result entry, extract result text and total_cost_usd. Handle errors: non-zero exit, is_error=true, malformed JSON, context cancellation. Test with fake claude shell script that outputs known JSON.
close_reason: Implemented in internal/runtime/claude/claude.go with 6 passing tests covering success, budget exceeded, CLI error, non-zero exit, context cancellation, and malformed JSON.

title: Design error handling strategy for v1 loop
description: At minimum the first version needs to handle: (1) CLI process exits non-zero (agent crash, budget exceeded), (2) git rebase --autosquash fails (merge conflict), (3) agent doesn't close any beads (implement step does nothing useful). Proposed approach: on any error, set stream to StatusPaused, record the error in the snapshot, and surface it for human intervention. Need to define error types, decide what's recoverable vs fatal, and implement the error paths in the loop.
close_reason: Added Error Handling section to ARCHITECTURE.md: LoopError type with 5 ErrorKind values, error detection table, loop pause-on-error behavior, restart semantics, TUI display. Updated Stream and Snapshot structs with error fields. Added errors.go to project layout.

title: test stream
close_reason: Stale test issues from prior sessions, no longer relevant

title: Data races: UI reads stream fields without mutex
description: The TUI's View() function accesses stream fields directly without holding the mutex, while the loop goroutine modifies them concurrently. Examples: renderDetail reads st.Snapshots, st.LastError, st.Iteration directly. renderDashboard reads st.Iteration and st.Guidance directly. These should use getter methods (like GetStatus/GetIteration do) that hold the RWMutex. Detectable with go test -race.
close_reason: Added GetSnapshots, GetLastError, GetGuidanceCount getters to Stream; updated all TUI render paths to use mutex-protected accessors

title: Channel layout for streams dashboard
description: Replace row-based stream list with vertical channel columns. See docs/plans/splendid-wishing-glacier.md. Each stream becomes a vertical column with iteration snapshots as rows. Includes toggle to fall back to existing list view.

Switch runtime from --output-format json to stream-json, parse streaming events into human-readable lines, store in a ring buffer on Stream, and display in an auto-scrolling TUI view. Show last output even when not running.

title: Record tech stack decision
description: Commit tech stack decision: Go + Bubble Tea. Evaluated Go, Ruby, Rust, Zig, TypeScript, Python. Go won for: goroutine concurrency model, mature Bubble Tea TUI framework, direct prior art (agent-deck, opencode), single binary output, strong subprocess management via os/exec.
close_reason: Closed

title: slog error output leaks into TUI alt screen
description: When an error occurs in the loop, slog.Error writes to os.Stderr. In TUI mode with tea.WithAltScreen, stderr output appears directly on the alt screen, overlapping with TUI content. The logger should be redirected to a file in TUI mode, or use tea.Program to route messages.
close_reason: Redirected slog output to <storeRoot>/streams.log in TUI mode. In runTUI, a log file is opened with append mode and a new slog handler is set to write there instead of stderr. Headless mode retains stderr logging as before.

title: Auto-initialize beads when creating streams in non-beads repos
description: When orch.Create runs bd create and it fails because beads isn't initialized in the target repo, streams should automatically run bd init first and retry. Currently the user just gets a cryptic 'exit status 1' error.
close_reason: Implemented proactive .beads/ check with TUI overlay prompting user for stealth vs normal mode. Uses --skip-hooks --quiet. Builds and tests pass.

title: Design pairing mode UX and loop integration
description: ModePairing exists as an enum value but is never consulted. Questions to resolve: (1) How does the user select pairing mode at stream creation? Add a mode toggle to the new-stream overlay, or a separate key binding? (2) Architecture says pairing forces TransitionMode to always return pause — where does this check live: in the loop, in each phase, or as a wrapper? (3) What does the TUI experience look like in pairing mode? Does the user see iteration output and explicitly approve each one? (4) Should pairing mode allow inline editing of the implement prompt before each iteration?
design:
## Pairing Mode Design

### Q1: How does the user select pairing mode?

**Mode toggle in the new-stream overlay.** Add a second line showing the current mode (default: Autonomous). The user presses Tab to toggle between Autonomous and Pairing before pressing Enter to create. The overlay passes the mode to orchestrator.Create().

**Mode switching mid-stream.** Add an `m` key binding on both dashboard and detail views. Pressing `m` toggles the selected stream's mode between Autonomous and Pairing. If the stream is running and switches to Pairing, the current iteration finishes normally, then the loop pauses before starting the next one. If switching to Autonomous while paused, the user still presses `s` to resume — mode switching doesn't auto-start.

Add a GetMode()/SetMode() accessor pair to Stream (follows existing pattern).

### Q2: Where does the pause-on-pairing check live?

**In the loop, at two points:**

1. **After convergence (existing TransitionMode check).** The loop already checks `phase.TransitionMode()` to decide auto-advance vs pause. Add a mode check: if `stream.Mode == ModePairing`, force `TransitionPause` regardless of what the phase returns. This is what ARCHITECTURE.md specifies.

2. **After StepGuidance, before continuing the next iteration.** This is the new behavior: in pairing mode, the loop pauses after EVERY iteration — not just at convergence. The user reviews each snapshot and explicitly resumes with `s`. Implementation: after draining guidance and incrementing the iteration counter, check `stream.GetMode() == ModePairing`. If true, set StatusPaused and return. The user resumes with `s`, which re-enters the loop for the next iteration.

The check lives in the loop (loop.go), not in each phase or as a wrapper. Phases don't need to know about pairing mode — they provide TransitionMode() as a preference, and the loop overrides it when pairing.

### Q3: What does the TUI look like in pairing mode?

**Dashboard:** Show mode indicator next to the stream status. Autonomous streams show nothing extra (it's the default). Pairing streams show a `[Pair]` badge. When paused-in-pairing (normal state between iterations), the status shows `Paused` as usual — the `[Pair]` badge distinguishes it from a convergence pause.

**Detail view:** Same as autonomous — shows the snapshot list and selected snapshot details. The key difference is behavioral: in pairing mode the stream pauses after each iteration, so the user naturally reviews snapshots one at a time.

**Workflow:** The user creates a stream in pairing mode. They press `s` to start the first iteration. The agent runs one implement-review cycle, produces a snapshot, and pauses. The user inspects the snapshot, optionally adds guidance with `g`, then presses `s` to run the next iteration. When satisfied, they press `m` to switch to autonomous and `s` to let it run freely.

**Help bar:** When the selected stream is in pairing mode, add `m: autonomous` to the help hints. When in autonomous mode, show `m: pairing`.

### Q4: Should pairing mode allow inline editing of the implement prompt?

**No, not in v1.** The guidance mechanism already serves this purpose — the user submits free-text direction via `g` that gets injected into the next implement prompt. Adding a prompt editor would duplicate this and add significant UI complexity (multi-line editor with pre-populated prompt text, cursor navigation, etc). If the user wants to steer the agent, they use guidance. If they want to change the fundamental phase behavior, they'd edit the phase prompts (future configurability).

### Implementation scope

Changes needed:
- **stream.go**: Add GetMode()/SetMode() accessors
- **loop.go**: Add pairing-mode pause check after StepGuidance; override TransitionMode on convergence when pairing
- **orchestrator.go**: Accept mode parameter in Create(); add ToggleMode() method
- **app.go**: Wire `m` key binding to orchestrator.ToggleMode(); pass mode from new-stream overlay
- **dashboard.go**: Show [Pair] badge; update help bar contextually
- **store.go**: Mode is already persisted (parseMode handles it)
close_reason: Design complete. Key decisions: (1) Mode toggle via Tab in new-stream overlay + m key to switch mid-stream. (2) Pause check lives in loop.go at two points: override TransitionMode on convergence, and pause after every iteration in pairing mode. (3) TUI shows [Pair] badge on dashboard, workflow is s-to-iterate with g-for-guidance between iterations. (4) No inline prompt editing in v1 — guidance mechanism covers the use case. See --design field for full writeup.

title: Deduplicate budgetRuntime between main.go and orchestrator.go
description: budgetRuntime is defined identically in both cmd/streams/main.go:147-159 and internal/orchestrator/orchestrator.go:277-289. Extract to a shared location (e.g. internal/runtime/budget.go) so both can reference it.
close_reason: Extracted BudgetRuntime to internal/runtime/budget.go with exported fields and Runtime interface for Inner. Updated both cmd/streams/main.go and internal/orchestrator/orchestrator.go to use runtime.BudgetRuntime, removing the duplicate local budgetRuntime types. The runtime package builds and vets cleanly. Note: go build ./... fails due to a pre-existing loop.Run signature mismatch in orchestrator.go unrelated to this change.

title: Auto-pause attach: epic
description: Improve attach UX so pressing 'a' auto-pauses the loop, waits for completion, and launches claude --resume. See docs/plans/1772836134-auto-pause-attach.md

title: Add done channel and Interrupt method to orchestrator
description: Add done map[string]chan struct{} to Orchestrator. In Start(), create a done channel and close(done) when the goroutine exits. Add Interrupt(id string) error that cancels the context and blocks on <-done. Stop() stays as-is (fire-and-forget). See docs/plans/1772836134-auto-pause-attach.md#step-1

title: Pre-assign session IDs at stream creation
description: Generate a UUID in orchestrator.Create(), store as stream.SessionID. Add SessionID field to runtime.Request. In claude.Runtime.Run(), pass --session-id when req.SessionID is set. In loop.Run(), set req.SessionID from stream on implement requests. This eliminates the 'no session ID yet' delay. See docs/plans/1772836134-auto-pause-attach.md#step-2

title: Update attach key handler with auto-pause flow
description: Replace the 'a' handler in updateDetail. If running: show 'Pausing...' status, fire Interrupt as tea.Cmd. On interruptDoneMsg: launch tea.ExecProcess(claude --resume). If not running + has session_id: launch directly. Add interruptDoneMsg type. Add attachedFromRunning bool to Model to track whether attach was from a running stream. See docs/plans/1772836134-auto-pause-attach.md#step-3

title: Add restart prompt after attach exit
description: When claudeExitMsg is received, if the stream was auto-paused (attachedFromRunning == true), show an overlay: 'Restart stream? (y/n)'. y calls orch.Start(id), n returns to detail view with stream paused. See docs/plans/1772836134-auto-pause-attach.md#step-4

title: Handle attach edge cases
description: Interrupt timeout: if the loop goroutine doesn't finish within ~10s, show an error and don't attach. Attach while creating: if stream is in Created status with no session, disable 'a'. Multiple rapid presses: ignore 'a' if already interrupting. See docs/plans/1772836134-auto-pause-attach.md#step-5

title: Review auto-pause attach implementation
description: Review the full diff for the auto-pause attach feature. Run tests. Verify with tmux testing per CLAUDE.md. Check for race conditions in the Interrupt/Start/done channel interactions.

title: Pairing mode: gear-shift mechanism
description: The Mode enum (Autonomous/Pairing) exists on Stream but is never set to ModePairing. Need to wire up: toggling mode from TUI, adjusting loop behavior when in pairing mode (TransitionMode always returns Pause), making the 'a' key a proper 'enter pairing' action rather than a one-shot attach. Session ID tracking is already in place from recent commits.

title: Implement disk persistence (store package)
description: Implement the store package per ARCHITECTURE.md: stream.json per stream (rewritten on checkpoint) and snapshots.jsonl (append-only). Load all streams on startup. Path: ~/.streams/streams/<stream-id>/.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add attach key handler with tea.ExecProcess
description: Add claudeExitMsg type and 'a' key handler in updateDetail (app.go). Guard: paused/stopped/errored only, must have session ID. Use tea.ExecProcess with exec.Command('claude', '--resume', sessionID) and cmd.Dir=worktree. Handle claudeExitMsg callback. Update detail help text (detail.go). Plan: docs/plans/valiant-gathering-fern.md Step 3
close_reason: Attach handler added with 'a' key in detail view. Uses tea.ExecProcess with claude --resume. Guarded on status and session ID presence. Help text updated.

title: Handle CLAUDE.md conflicts with phase system prompts
description: Spawned Claude CLI sessions automatically load the target repo's CLAUDE.md. If that file contains instructions that contradict phase prompts (e.g. 'always commit', 'never use beads', specific git workflows), the agent could misbehave. Options: (1) add override instruction in --append-system-prompt saying 'these instructions take priority over CLAUDE.md', (2) use a flag to skip user rules if one exists, (3) accept the risk and document it. Need to decide approach and implement it in the prompt templates.
close_reason: Decided on Option A: targeted override in --append-system-prompt. Added shared orchestrator override block to ARCHITECTURE.md that overrides operational workflow instructions while preserving project coding conventions. claudeMdExcludes available as escape hatch.

title: Wire up main entry point with CLI flags and signal handling
description: cmd/streams/main.go. Headless entry point (no TUI). Flags: --task, --dir (workdir), --beads-parent (optional — creates parent issue if not provided), --max-iterations (default 10), --max-budget-per-step (default 2.00). If no --beads-parent, run bd create to create one. Creates a single stream with pipeline=['coding'], creates git worktree, wires up Runtime + Loop, runs to convergence or max iterations. Signal handling: Ctrl+C → context cancellation → kills subprocess → clean exit. Logging via slog to stderr. Exit code 0 on convergence, 1 on error, 2 on max iterations reached.
close_reason: Implemented cmd/streams/main.go with all required flags, signal handling, worktree creation, beads parent creation, and exit codes. Added maxIterations to loop.Run.

title: Fix stale bd prime output at session start
description: The bd prime --stealth output injected by the SessionStart hook contains an outdated session close protocol with git push, which contradicts the global CLAUDE.md NEVER USE GIT PUSH rule and the deny rule in settings.json. The current bd prime --stealth output correctly uses stealth mode (bd sync --flush-only). Verify in a fresh session whether this is already fixed by a recent bd update. If not, investigate why the hook produces different output than running bd prime --stealth directly.
close_reason: Already fixed. bd prime --stealth correctly outputs bd sync --flush-only only, with no git push. Confirmed in current session.

title: Phase factory to instantiate MacroPhase by name
description: The orchestrator hardcodes &loop.CodingPhase{} (orchestrator.go:186). Add a factory function that maps pipeline name strings ("plan", "decompose", "coding") to MacroPhase instances. This is needed by pipeline advancement to instantiate the correct phase when PipelineIndex advances.
close_reason: Added loop.NewPhase() factory function in phase.go that maps pipeline name strings to MacroPhase instances. Orchestrator.Start() now uses it instead of hardcoding CodingPhase. Switch statement is ready for new phases (plan, decompose) to be added.

title: Implement new stream creation flow in TUI
description: The 'n' key binding in the dashboard is stubbed. Add a text input prompt (or overlay similar to guidance) that collects the task description, then calls orch.Create(task) to create the stream.
close_reason: Implemented text input overlay for new stream creation

title: TUI deadlocks when starting a stream (s key)
description: Pressing 's' to start a stream causes the TUI to completely freeze. orchestrator.Start() calls o.emit() synchronously within the Bubble Tea Update() handler. emit() calls p.Send() which tries to deliver an event to the program, but the event loop is blocked processing the current keystroke. This creates a deadlock. No keys work afterward, including q and ctrl+c (ctrl+c does work via OS signal but only because Bubble Tea handles it separately).
close_reason: Made emit() async with go sink.Send(e) to prevent deadlock when called from Bubble Tea's Update() handler

title: Force-advance pipeline from TUI
description: If a phase gets stuck (e.g. plan keeps cycling without converging), the user has no way to skip it and move to the next phase. Add a TUI key binding to force-advance the pipeline index. The guidance design doc in ARCHITECTURE.md mentions this as a future action.

title: Solve step bead ordering without native beads support
description: The decompose phase creates step beads with titles like 'Step 1: ...', 'Step 2: ...'. But beads doesn't have a native ordering field. The implement agent sorts by title convention. This is fragile — if the decompose review causes renumbering, ordering could break. Options: (1) rely on title prefix convention and accept the fragility, (2) check if beads supports an order/sequence field, (3) track step ordering in the stream state as a separate ordered list of bead IDs, (4) use beads dependency chains (step 2 depends on step 1) to encode ordering. Need to pick an approach.
close_reason: Decided on metadata.sequence approach: decompose agent sets {"sequence":N} on each step bead, Go loop sorts by it and injects ordered list into coding prompt. Updated ARCHITECTURE.md prompts, macro-phase descriptions, and added design decision.

title: hello world
close_reason: Stale test issues from prior sessions, no longer relevant

title: Consider installing Claude beads plugin
description: bd doctor suggests installing the beads Claude plugin for slash command support. This would enable /beads commands directly in Claude Code. Install with: /plugin marketplace add steveyegge/beads && /plugin install beads. This is optional but could improve the workflow.
close_reason: Deferred to user — plugin install requires Claude Code slash commands: /plugin marketplace add steveyegge/beads && /plugin install beads. This is optional (P4 backlog).

title: Support configurable pipeline in stream creation
description: orchestrator.Create() hardcodes Pipeline to ["coding"] (orchestrator.go:122). Should support the full plan→decompose→coding pipeline and other configurations. Also, headless mode in main.go:109 hardcodes CodingPhase instead of using the stream's pipeline. Need to: (1) add a Pipeline field to orchestrator.Config or accept it as a Create parameter, (2) fix headless mode to respect the stream's configured pipeline by using the phase factory.
close_reason: Added Pipeline field to orchestrator.Config, Create() uses it with coding default, headless mode uses stream pipeline with NewPhase factory, added --pipeline CLI flag for comma-separated phase names

title: Add cleanup option to stream deletion
description: Delete stream confirmation should let user choose to also clean up git branch and beads issue, or leave them intact
close_reason: Added cleanup bool param to Orchestrator.Delete. When true, deletes git branch (git branch -D) and beads issue (bd close + bd delete). TUI overlay updated: y=cleanup, k=keep, esc=cancel. Verified overlay rendering via tmux.

title: Fix JSONL sync mismatch
description: Dolt has 5 issues but issues.jsonl only has 1. The git-tracked JSONL export is out of date. Fix by running bd sync --flush-only to reconcile.
close_reason: Fixed by running bd sync --flush-only. JSONL now has 13 entries covering all issues.

title: Implement orchestrator package
description: Multi-stream manager: manages stream collection (create/list/get), starts/stops loop goroutines, routes guidance, sends LoopEvent to TUI via tea.Program.Send(), persists via store on checkpoint.
close_reason: Closed

title: Build minimal prototype
description: Build a minimal working prototype: one stream, one loop iteration, basic TUI. Stack: Go + Bubble Tea. Should demonstrate the core flow: create a stream with a task, run one loop iteration (implement → self-review → checkpoint), display result in TUI. Depends on architecture design (streams-8aw).
close_reason: Closed

title: test-parse-check
close_reason: test issue created to check bd output format

title: Pipeline advancement on phase convergence
description: When a phase converges, the loop currently pauses and returns (loop.go:151-155). Instead, it should check phase.TransitionMode(): if auto-advance, increment stream.PipelineIndex, instantiate the next phase via the phase factory, reset iteration counter, and continue. If pause (or pipeline exhausted), pause as today. Depends on phase factory existing.
close_reason: Implemented auto-advance in loop.go convergence block. Run() now accepts PhaseFactory; on convergence with TransitionAutoAdvance, instantiates next phase, resets iteration, continues. Pauses when pipeline exhausted or TransitionPause. Three new tests cover auto-advance, exhausted pipeline, and pause-no-advance.

title: End-to-end integration test for full pipeline
description: No test exercises the full plan→decompose→coding pipeline with a mock runtime. Need a test that creates a stream, runs through all three phases with mock Runtime/BeadsQuerier/GitQuerier, and verifies: pipeline advances correctly, snapshots are produced for each phase, convergence detection works across phase transitions, error handling pauses the stream correctly.
close_reason: Added 4 end-to-end pipeline tests in pipeline_test.go covering: (1) full 3-phase plan->decompose->coding pipeline with auto-advance, (2) error mid-pipeline pauses stream with correct state, (3) checkpoint callback invocation count, (4) gate results populated from review text keywords. All tests pass.

title: happy path test
close_reason: Stale test issues from prior sessions, no longer relevant

title: Debug tail view: stream-json output not appearing
description: The tail view (t key in detail view) was implemented in commit 6d7af39 but output is not showing up. Likely the stream-json NDJSON parsing in internal/runtime/claude/claude.go runStreaming() is not matching the actual CLI output format. Need to: 1) Capture actual stream-json output from claude CLI to see real event structure 2) Fix the streamEvent/contentBlock parsing to match 3) Verify output flows through OnOutput callback -> stream.AppendOutput -> tail view. Key files: internal/runtime/claude/claude.go (runStreaming), internal/runtime/claude/response.go (event types), internal/loop/loop.go (OnOutput wiring), internal/ui/tail.go (rendering).
notes: ROOT CAUSE: Two issues found: (1) Missing --verbose flag causes CLI to error: 'output-format=stream-json requires --verbose'. (2) Event format mismatch: actual stream-json uses type 'stream_event' wrapping API events (content_block_delta etc), not type 'assistant' with message.content blocks. FIX: Add --verbose and --include-partial-messages flags, handle stream_event type with text buffering for line-based output.
close_reason: Fixed two bugs: (1) added missing --verbose flag required by CLI for stream-json output, (2) added --include-partial-messages and stream_event handler for real-time text/tool streaming with line buffering. Tests added and passing.

title: Define guidance injection mechanics
description: The architecture says 'drain the guidance queue and incorporate into next iteration' but doesn't specify how concretely. Proposed: guidance text gets prepended to the next implement step's prompt, wrapped in a section like 'The user has provided the following guidance: ...'. After being consumed, guidance is cleared from the queue but preserved in the snapshot for history. Need to also consider: should guidance be able to force a phase transition? Should it be able to override the system prompt? Can guidance inject into the review step or only implement?
close_reason: Designed guidance injection mechanics: implement-only injection, additive (not override), content direction (not meta-control), loop-level concern. Added GuidanceConsumed to Snapshot. Documented data flow, prompt format, timing, and boundaries.

title: Design git branch strategy and working directory isolation
description: Two related decisions needed before coding. (1) Git branch: The coding phase makes commits and runs autosquash rebases. On what branch? Options: stream creates a new branch from HEAD, user specifies a branch, or work on current branch. Autosquash needs a known base commit — the stream probably needs to record a BaseSHA at creation time. (2) Working directory: Does the stream create a git worktree or work in-place? In-place is simpler but blocks the user from using the repo during a run. Affects the CLI runtime's WorkDir config. Both decisions affect multi-stream later but need answering for single-stream v1.
notes: Decisions: (1) Stream creates dedicated branch streams/<stream-id> from HEAD, records BaseSHA for autosquash rebase target. (2) Git worktree at .streams/worktrees/<stream-id>. Stream struct adds BaseSHA, Branch, WorkTree fields. Worktree created via git worktree add, cleaned up via git worktree remove on stream completion.
close_reason: Decided: worktree at .streams/worktrees/<stream-id>, branch streams/<stream-id>, BaseSHA records rebase target. Documented in ARCHITECTURE.md.

title: Implement Plan macro-phase
description: Add PlanPhase implementing the MacroPhase interface. Full prompt specs are in ARCHITECTURE.md under 'Plan phase — implement prompt' and 'Plan phase — review prompt'. Implement produces/revises plan.md, review files child issues for improvements. Tools: implement gets Bash,Read,Edit,Write,Glob,Grep; review gets Bash,Read,Glob,Grep. TransitionMode returns auto-advance.
close_reason: PlanPhase implemented with iteration-aware prompts, no-op BeforeReview, AutoAdvance transition, registered in factory. All tests pass.

title: Populate snapshot DiffStat, CommitSHAs, and beads delta fields
description: Snapshots currently leave DiffStat, CommitSHAs, BeadsClosed, and BeadsOpened empty. After the implement step, capture: git diff --stat for DiffStat, git log for CommitSHAs (commits made this iteration), and bead ID deltas (compare open children before/after implement for BeadsClosed, before/after review for BeadsOpened). The before/after counts already exist in the loop — this extends them to capture IDs.
close_reason: Implemented: ListOpenChildren replaces CountOpenChildren for ID-based tracking, GitQuerier captures HEAD/DiffStat/CommitsBetween, loop.Run populates all snapshot fields (DiffStat, CommitSHAs, BeadsClosed, BeadsOpened). New TestRunSnapshotPopulatesFields validates all four fields.

title: Add mid-iteration checkpointing and TUI events
description: The loop only persists state when the goroutine exits (orchestrator.checkpoint called once in the Start goroutine). Per ARCHITECTURE.md, checkpointing should happen at StepCheckpoint within each iteration. Also, no EventCheckpoint events are emitted during iterations, so the TUI doesn't update in real-time. Need to: (1) add a CheckpointFunc callback parameter to loop.Run, (2) call it at StepCheckpoint each iteration, (3) have the orchestrator's callback persist state and emit EventCheckpoint to the TUI.
close_reason: Added onCheckpoint callback parameter to loop.Run. Orchestrator passes a callback that persists state via o.checkpoint and emits EventCheckpoint. Headless mode and all tests pass nil. All tests pass, build succeeds.

title: Verify Bash auto-approval with --allowedTools and --permission-mode
description: The architecture specifies --allowedTools 'Bash,...' and --permission-mode acceptEdits. But acceptEdits only auto-approves file edits. The agent needs to run bd and git commands via Bash without hanging for permission. Need to verify: does --allowedTools Bash combined with --permission-mode acceptEdits auto-approve Bash invocations? If not, we may need a different permission mode or --dangerously-skip-permissions. This is critical for autonomous operation — a hung permission prompt would silently block the loop.
close_reason: Verified: acceptEdits does NOT auto-approve Bash. Updated ARCHITECTURE.md to use --permission-mode dontAsk instead, which silently blocks non-allowed tools without hanging.

title: Invalid pipeline phase accepted at stream creation
description: Streams are created with whatever pipeline phases are passed in --pipeline, even invalid ones. The validation happens at Start time (NewPhase returns error), but by then the stream is already persisted. Should validate pipeline phase names during Create.
close_reason: Added ValidatePipeline function; Create() now rejects invalid phase names before persisting

title: Design quality gate evaluation
description: The Gate interface exists (gates.go) but is never called and has no implementations. Questions to resolve: (1) How are gates parsed from review text? The architecture says 'parsed from review output' but the prompts don't ask the reviewer to structure output for gate extraction. (2) Should gates be hard-coded checklist items or LLM-evaluated? (3) Do gate failures block convergence or are they advisory? (4) The 4 default gates (pattern conformance, simplicity, readability, hindsight) — are these separate LLM calls, string-matching on review text, or something else? Propose a concrete design.
close_reason: Gates as spec'd duplicate the review agent's work. The review already evaluates pattern conformance, simplicity, readability, and correctness, and files issues for failures. Gates would just be a lossy summary of that. The Gate interface stays unimplemented until a concrete differentiating use case emerges (e.g., automated/deterministic checks like test pass or linter clean, or user-defined custom criteria).

title: Implement loop engine
description: Create internal/loop/loop.go. Main function: Run(ctx context.Context, s *stream.Stream, phase MacroPhase, rt runtime.Runtime, beads BeadsQuerier) — no return value, outcome reflected in stream state (StatusPaused+Converged, StatusPaused+LastError, or StatusStopped). Loop flow per iteration: (1) check ctx.Done, (2) fetch ordered steps via beads.FetchOrderedSteps, (3) StepImplement — count open children before, call rt.Run with implement prompt, count after, check ErrNoProgress on iteration>0, (4) StepAutosquash — call phase.BeforeReview, (5) StepReview — call rt.Run with review prompt, count open children after, (6) convergence check via phase.IsConverged, (7) StepCheckpoint — build Snapshot and append to stream. On convergence set Converged+StatusPaused. Check ctx.Err() after each rt.Run to distinguish cancellation from runtime errors. Include orchestratorRules constant (CLAUDE.md override block from ARCHITECTURE.md). Helpers: buildRequest (constructs runtime.Request with allowedTools, appendSystemPrompt=orchestratorRules, permissionMode=dontAsk), classifyError (checks for 'budget' keyword), recordError (sets LastError, StatusPaused, appends error snapshot, logs via slog).
close_reason: Implemented loop.go with Run, buildRequest, classifyError, recordError, appendGuidanceSection

title: Document Claude CLI JSON output format for runtime parsing
description: We need to know the exact shape of 'claude -p --output-format json' output to build the runtime parser. Fields needed: response text, cost/usage info, possibly session ID and tool call details. This should be validated empirically (run the CLI and inspect output) before building internal/runtime/claude/claude.go. Check if the format is documented or if we need to reverse-engineer it from actual output.
close_reason: Documented in internal/runtime/claude/response.go with cliResult struct and package docs. Validated empirically: success has result field, budget error uses subtype error_max_budget_usd with exit 0, max-turns uses subtype error_max_turns.

title: Mid-flight guidance cancellation
description: Currently guidance submitted while the implement step is running sits in the queue until StepGuidance at the end of the iteration. ARCHITECTURE.md documents a future enhancement: cancel the running CLI process via context cancellation and restart the iteration with guidance injected, giving the user immediate steering. Discuss: is the latency of waiting for the current iteration acceptable, or is this worth the complexity?

title: Record project direction: design-first, vertical slices
description: Prototype (streams-t2v) and debugging (streams-291) work was scrapped. New approach: iterate on architecture and design more thoroughly, then build features as vertical slices (end-to-end through all layers). Record this decision so future sessions have context.
close_reason: Decision recorded: scrapped prototype, taking design-first approach with vertical slices
